title: Obituary search job response (Finder → Matcher)
description: The GET /jobs/{jobId} response body. The matcher treats every field as UNTRUSTED (scraped from external sites) and re-validates before use. All free-text and collection fields are bounded so this schema is a safe validation gate against a hostile/buggy finder. Producer obligation: results MUST only contain keys that were in the request's candidates; the matcher MUST drop any unrequested key.

allOf.description: A done job MUST carry results (possibly an empty object); queued/running/failed need not.

schemaVersion.description: Major contract version (major-only versioning; there is no minor channel). The matcher MUST reject an unknown major.

state.description: Job lifecycle state. `results` is only meaningful once `done`.

startedAt.description: When the finder began processing the job.

finishedAt.description: When the job reached a terminal state (done/failed).

results.description: Per-person results, keyed by the request's personId. Present when state is `done`. A personId with no notices but a coverage report means "searched, nothing found" — distinct from a missing key. The matcher MUST ignore any key not present in the submitted candidates.

warnings.description: Non-fatal, human-readable diagnostics (e.g. a portal that failed). Never a substitute for per-person coverage. MUST be a sanitised summary — never a stack trace, internal path, or raw upstream response body.

notices.description: Death notices found for this person across ALL portals (not stopped at the first hit), deduplicated by normalised URL. A real person rarely has many notices; the cap is a defensive ceiling, not an expected volume.

coverage.description: Which portals were actually consulted for this person, so the matcher can tell "nothing found" (ok, 0) apart from "could not search" (failed). REQUIRED to drive a correct negative-result memory. Producer obligation: every notice's `source` MUST appear here with status "ok", and a portal's `noticeCount` MUST equal the number of that portal's notices in `notices[]`.

Notice.description: One scraped death notice. Only name/url/source/fetchedAt are guaranteed; every other field is best-effort and may be null/absent depending on finder capabilities.

noticeType.description: Kind of notice. `unknown` when the finder cannot classify it.

name.description: Name as printed in the notice, plain text.

birth.description: Birth date from the notice (the "*" field), ISO 8601 date, or null if absent.

death.description: Death date from the notice (the "†" field), proleptic-Gregorian YYYY-MM-DD, or null. This is the ANSWER the suggestion delivers; the matcher never scores points for its mere presence. Best-effort: a notice yielding only a month/year (no day) MUST set this null — the confirm-gate needs a day-precise date, so a fabricated day is forbidden.

place.description: Place/region stated in the notice, or null.

cemetery.description: Burial cemetery free-text (e.g. "Waldfriedhof Musterstadt"), or null.

age.description: Stated age at death ("im Alter von N"), or null. The finder MUST drop an implausible scraped value rather than emit it, so one misprint never fails the whole response.

funeralDate.description: Funeral/burial date if stated, ISO 8601 date, or null.

relatives.description: Relatives named in the notice (used by the matcher's relative scorer).

url.description: Canonical URL of the notice. MUST be http or https, carry a host, and contain no userinfo. The matcher MUST still reject non-public/internal hosts before any server-side fetch (SSRF) and render the link with rel="noopener noreferrer", never auto-fetching it.

source.description: Portal id that produced this notice. MUST match one of this finder's capabilities portal ids and MUST appear in this person's coverage[] with status "ok".

fetchedAt.description: When the finder fetched the notice.

name.description: Relative's name as printed, plain text.

relation.description: Relationship to the deceased if the finder could infer it; null/absent otherwise.

portal.description: Portal id (matches a capabilities portal id).

status.description: ok = searched successfully (see noticeCount); failed = could not search (error/timeout, or a requested portal that is down) — the matcher MUST NOT record a negative result; skipped = deliberately not searched (the finder's own perf/negative cache, a requested portal the finder does not offer, or a portal not in the requested set) — the matcher MUST NOT treat this as a confirmed miss either. A requested portal the finder cannot serve MUST appear here (skipped/failed), never be silently omitted.

noticeCount.description: Number of notices this portal contributed for this person (after dedup). 0 with status ok = genuine "nothing found". Bounded by the per-person notices ceiling; NON-AUTHORITATIVE — the matcher MUST NOT size allocations from it, only cross-check against the actual notices.

message.description: Human-readable detail, especially for status failed/skipped. Sanitised summary only — never a stack trace, internal path, or raw upstream body.

Counts.description: Optional job-level tallies for status display only — NON-AUTHORITATIVE. Per-person truth lives in each PersonResult's coverage[]; the matcher MUST NOT drive logic from these counts.
